documentation.value:
Specifies the display behavior of an element.

Values: `block`, `inline`, `inline-block`, `flex`, `grid`, `none`, `contents`, `inline-flex`, `inline-grid`, `flow-root`, `table`, `list-item`.

Sets how an element is positioned in a document. Works with `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, and `z-index`.

Values: `static`, `relative`, `absolute`, `fixed`, `sticky`.

Shorthand for `flex-grow`, `flex-shrink`, and `flex-basis`.

Syntax: `flex: <grow> <shrink> <basis>;`

Examples:
- `flex: 1;` — grow to fill space
- `flex: 0 0 auto;` — don't grow or shrink
- `flex: 1 1 0%;` — distribute space evenly

Defines the columns of a grid container.

Examples:
- `grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;`
- `grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);`
- `grid-template-columns: minmax(200px, 1fr) 3fr;`
- `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto;`

Defines the rows of a grid container.

Examples:
- `grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto;`
- `grid-template-rows: repeat(3, minmax(100px, auto));`

Sets the margin area on all four sides.

Syntax: `margin: <top> <right> <bottom> <left>;`

Shorthand patterns:
- `margin: 10px;` — all sides
- `margin: 10px 20px;` — vertical | horizontal
- `margin: 10px 20px 30px;` — top | horizontal | bottom
- `margin: 10px 20px 30px 40px;` — top | right | bottom | left
- `margin: 0 auto;` — center horizontally

Sets the padding area on all four sides.

Syntax: `padding: <top> <right> <bottom> <left>;`

Accepts length, percentage, or `0`. Does not accept negative values or `auto`.

Sets the foreground color of text and text decorations.

Accepts: named colors, hex (`#fff`), `rgb()`, `rgba()`, `hsl()`, `hsla()`, `oklch()`, `lab()`, `lch()`, `color()`, `currentColor`, `inherit`, `transparent`.

Shorthand for `background-color`, `background-image`, `background-position`, `background-size`, `background-repeat`, `background-origin`, `background-clip`, and `background-attachment`.

Examples:
- `background: #fff;`
- `background: url('bg.png') no-repeat center/cover;`
- `background: linear-gradient(to right, #000, #fff);`

Shorthand for `border-width`, `border-style`, and `border-color`.

Syntax: `border: <width> <style> <color>;`

Styles: `none`, `solid`, `dashed`, `dotted`, `double`, `groove`, `ridge`, `inset`, `outset`.

Shorthand for `font-style`, `font-variant`, `font-weight`, `font-size`/`line-height`, and `font-family`.

Syntax: `font: [style] [variant] [weight] size[/line-height] family;`

Example: `font: italic bold 16px/1.5 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;`

Shorthand for `animation-name`, `animation-duration`, `animation-timing-function`, `animation-delay`, `animation-iteration-count`, `animation-direction`, `animation-fill-mode`, and `animation-play-state`.

Syntax: `animation: name duration timing-function delay iteration-count direction fill-mode;`

Example: `animation: fadeIn 0.3s ease-in-out forwards;`

Shorthand for `transition-property`, `transition-duration`, `transition-timing-function`, and `transition-delay`.

Syntax: `transition: property duration timing-function delay;`

Examples:
- `transition: all 0.3s ease;`
- `transition: opacity 0.2s, transform 0.3s ease-out;`

Applies a 2D or 3D transformation to an element.

Functions: `translate()`, `translateX()`, `translateY()`, `rotate()`, `scale()`, `scaleX()`, `scaleY()`, `skew()`, `skewX()`, `skewY()`, `matrix()`, `perspective()`, `translate3d()`, `rotate3d()`, `scale3d()`.

Sets the z-order of a positioned element and its descendants. Only works on elements with `position` other than `static`.

Values: integer (positive or negative), `auto`.

Sets what to do when content overflows the element's box.

Values: `visible`, `hidden`, `clip`, `scroll`, `auto`.

Also: `overflow-x`, `overflow-y` for individual axis control.

Sets the type of mouse cursor when hovering over an element.

Common values: `pointer`, `default`, `text`, `move`, `not-allowed`, `grab`, `grabbing`, `crosshair`, `wait`, `progress`, `help`, `zoom-in`, `zoom-out`, `none`, `url()` for custom cursors.

Sets the opacity level for an element. Affects the entire element including contents.

Range: `0` (fully transparent) to `1` (fully opaque).

Note: Unlike `visibility: hidden`, opacity `0` still occupies space and receives events.

Defines how the width and height of an element are calculated.

- `content-box` (default): Width/height only include content
- `border-box`: Width/height include padding and border

Best practice: `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }`

Matches when the user interacts with an element via a pointing device (mouse hover).

Specificity: adds (0, 1, 0) to the selector.

Example: `a:hover { color: blue; text-decoration: underline; }`

Matches when an element has received focus (keyboard tab, click, or programmatic focus).

Best practice: Always provide visible `:focus` styles for accessibility. Consider `:focus-visible` for keyboard-only focus.

Creates a pseudo-element as the last child of the selected element. Requires `content` property.

Commonly used for decorative elements, clearfixes, and overlays.

Matches elements based on their position among siblings.

Syntax: `:nth-child(An+B [of S]?)`

Examples:
- `:nth-child(2)` — second child
- `:nth-child(odd)` / `:nth-child(2n+1)` — odd children
- `:nth-child(even)` / `:nth-child(2n)` — even children
- `:nth-child(3n)` — every third child
- `:nth-child(-n+3)` — first three children

Matches the first child element. Equivalent to `:nth-child(1)`.

Example: `li:first-child { font-weight: bold; }`

Applies styles conditionally based on media queries.

Media types: `all`, `screen`, `print`
Media features: `width`, `height`, `aspect-ratio`, `orientation`, `resolution`, `prefers-color-scheme`, `prefers-reduced-motion`, `hover`, `pointer`.

Logical operators: `and`, `not`, `only`, `,` (or).

Imports a stylesheet into the current stylesheet. Must appear before all other rules except `@charset` and `@layer`.

Syntax:
- `@import url('file.css');`
- `@import url('print.css') print;`
- `@import url('mobile.css') screen and (max-width: 768px);`
- `@import 'file.css' layer(name);`

completions.label: @font-face

documentation.value:
Defines a custom font to be used in the document.

Descriptors: `font-family`, `src`, `font-weight`, `font-style`, `font-display`, `unicode-range`, `font-stretch`, `font-variant`, `font-feature-settings`.

`font-display` values: `auto`, `block`, `swap`, `fallback`, `optional`.

Feature query — applies styles only if the browser supports a given CSS feature.

Examples:
- `@supports (display: grid) { ... }`
- `@supports not (gap: 1rem) { ... }`
- `@supports (display: grid) and (gap: 1rem) { ... }`
- `@supports selector(:has()) { ... }`

Declares cascade layers to control the order of specificity.

Syntax:
- `@layer name;` — declare layer order
- `@layer name { ... }` — assign rules to layer
- `@layer name1, name2, name3;` — define layer order
- `@import url('lib.css') layer(lib);`

Layers declared earlier have lower priority.

Inserts the value of a CSS custom property.

Syntax: `var(--property-name, fallback-value)`

Example:
```css
:root { --primary: #3490dc; }
.btn { color: var(--primary, blue); }
```
Fallback can itself use `var()`: `var(--a, var(--b, #000))`.

completions.label: Flexbox Center

documentation.value:
Centers child elements both horizontally and vertically using Flexbox.

This is the most common centering technique in modern CSS.

completions.label: Grid Layout

documentation.value: Creates a responsive grid layout using `auto-fill` and `minmax()` for fluid column sizing without media queries.

Mobile-first media query for tablet and above (768px). Common breakpoints:
- 480px: Mobile landscape
- 768px: Tablet
- 1024px: Desktop
- 1280px: Large desktop

Defines a CSS custom property (CSS variable) on the `:root` pseudo-class for global usage.

Custom properties are inherited and can be overridden in nested scopes.
Uses the `prefers-color-scheme` media feature to apply dark mode styles based on user system preference.

Performs calculations to determine CSS property values. Supports `+`, `-`, `*`, `/` and can mix units.

Examples:
- `width: calc(100% - 2rem);`
- `font-size: calc(1rem + 0.5vw);`
- `height: calc(100vh - var(--header-height));`

Clamps a value between a minimum and maximum.

Syntax: `clamp(MIN, VAL, MAX)`

Example: `font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem);` — responsive font that never goes below 1rem or above 2rem.

Sets the gap (gutter) between rows and columns in flex and grid layouts.

Shorthand for `row-gap` and `column-gap`.

- `gap: 1rem;` — uniform gap
- `gap: 1rem 2rem;` — row gap | column gap

completions.label: Visually Hidden

documentation.value: Hides an element visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Preferred over `display: none` for accessibility.
